title: Someone else's page inside yours
why.no-iframe: There is no embedded window. The tag is <iframe> and a whole foreign page lives inside it.
why.no-src: The tag is there, but it does not say which page to show. src is missing.
why.no-title: There is no title. A blind person reaches the embedded window and hears… nothing. They do not know if it is a map, a video, or an advert. One attribute solves that.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed. <iframe> has a closing tag.
blocks.0.text: You want to show where to come and get the bike. A map. Except a map is not something you build with HTML — it is a whole site in its own right.
blocks.1.text: You do not need to build it. You need to borrow it.
blocks.4.text: <iframe> is a window. Inside it lives another whole page — not yours, someone else's. It has its own code, its own styles, its own life.
blocks.5.text: That is where maps, YouTube videos and payment fields come from.
blocks.7.text: That is the important part. You cannot touch what is inside. You cannot style it, you cannot read it, you cannot change it.
blocks.8.text: And the reverse: it cannot touch you. The two pages are separated by a wall.
blocks.9.text: That wall is deliberate. Without it, every embedded window would be reading your passwords.
blocks.11.text: You are embedding someone else's code in your page. If the site on the other side turns malicious, the person thinks they are still with you.
blocks.12.text: There is a sandbox attribute that ties its hands — it forbids running code, opening windows, writing to disk. Use it when you embed something you do not fully trust.
blocks.13.text: The rule: only embed sites you would hand your house keys to.
blocks.15.text: Did you notice the title in the example? It is not decoration.
blocks.16.text: A blind person reaches the embedded window and hears… nothing. They do not know if it is a map, a video, an advert or emptiness. They do not know whether to enter it.
blocks.17.text: title tells them what is inside — before they decide whether to spend time on it.
blocks.18.text: The same as alt on images: one invisible attribute, without which half the people are blind to your page.
blocks.19.text: Your turn. Embed something and do not forget to give it a name.
